key_insight: A non-overlapping Schwarz FE–neural operator framework couples Point-DeepONet and finite elements through Neumann-Dirichlet exchange to accelerate solid mechanics on irregular domains without sacrificing mechanical consistency.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate-assisted simulation for nonlinear, dynamic, and geometrically complex solid mechanics problems where standard FE solves are too costly.
method: Replace overlapping Dirichlet-Dirichlet coupling with non-overlapping Schwarz Neumann-Dirichlet traction transfer.
Use Point-DeepONet on unstructured FE point clouds to support non-convex and irregular subdomains.
Derive strain and stress analytically from displacement operators to reduce parameters and enforce physics.
evidence_signal: The framework was validated on three benchmarks—static linear elasticity, quasi-static hyperelasticity, and elastodynamics—across regular and irregular geometries, with reduced inner Schwarz iterations and stable error over long time horizons.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate interface traction exchange and training stability of the operator model, especially for strongly nonlinear or highly dynamic subdomains.
paper_title: A Non-Overlapping Schwarz Hybrid Finite Element-Neural Operator Framework for Solid Mechanics on Irregular Domains
